task: What is the right next feature to add to the node-ts-starter template?

retrieve.note: No prior pattern existed for this project/task at time of write; this record seeds future retrieval.

judge.rationale: Decision is internally consistent with the repo's own stated scope-control rule in CLAUDE.md, adds zero new runtime dependencies, and every accepted/rejected item is traceable to an explicit reason rather than taste.

distill.approach: Evaluate every candidate addition against a single guiding principle (zero new runtime deps + universal value to any template consumer) before accepting.

accepted.spec: pnpm/action-setup@v4 + actions/setup-node@v4 (cache: pnpm); pnpm install --frozen-lockfile; single Node 22.x job running tsc --noEmit -> pnpm build -> pnpm test; triggers on push/PR to main
~40 lines: CI badge, quickstart (install/dev/test/build), scripts table, one line noting no framework included by design

rejected.reason: devDependency weight + bikeshedding; tsc --noEmit already covers correctness at this size; revisit only if a team style guide becomes needed
contradicts this repo's CLAUDE.md rule: add Express/Fastify only when a real endpoint is needed
no deployment target exists yet; premature ops concern
YAGNI; nothing in the codebase reads env vars currently

distill.guiding_principle: This is a minimal starter template: weigh every addition against bloat; accept only additions with zero new runtime dependencies and universal value to any consumer of the template.

consolidate.revisit_trigger: Re-run this evaluation only if inputs change: a real deployment target appears (revisit Dockerfile), env vars get introduced (revisit env-loading), or a team style guide is requested (revisit ESLint/Prettier).
